label: gguf_q4km_int8_kv_correctness_guard

decision.default_behavior: BF16 KV remains the GGUF default. Explicit int8_per_token_head is correctness-admitted only for rounded max contexts <=8192 where the BF16 mirror is retained.
decision.long_context_behavior: Rounded max contexts >8192 are blocked by default; set HIPENGINE_GGUF_INT8_KV_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_LONG=1 only to reproduce old INT8-only capacity diagnostics.
decision.reason: The INT8-only no-mirror GGUF path failed a BF16-vs-INT8 logit gate even at 4K on W7900, so it is not safe to expose silently as a correctness path.

4096.note: Compared externally against /tmp/gguf-int8-correctness-bf16.json: KL=0 and max_abs=0 for prefill and first decode after BF16 mirror.

512.note: Compared externally against /tmp/gguf-int8-correctness-bf16.json: KL=0 and max_abs=0 for prefill and first decode after BF16 mirror.

correctness_failures: prompt=4096: KL/top1 gate failed: kl_mean=0.275781 <= 0.05, top1=0.5 >= 0.9

notes: Primitive HIP INT8 writer/decode accuracy is green on synthetic contexts 64 and 520, so the GGUF rejection is model/logit quality of the current per-token/head INT8-only route, not a basic wrapper failure.
The 4K no-mirror run used max_sequence_length=131202 to force the same no-BF16-mirror path that long contexts would use.

Earlier 128K and 32K same-script W7900 attempts timed out before artifact write; they are superseded by the faster 4K no-mirror rejection because the unmirrored path already fails the relaxed logit gate.

The no-mirror logit-gate command was captured immediately before adding the fail-fast guard; with current code, set HIPENGINE_GGUF_INT8_KV_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_LONG=1 to reproduce the same INT8-only path instead of the new admission error.
